sections.title: Overview
sections.content: **Microsoft Outlook (Mail)** connector — `CONNECTOR_TYPE = "outlook_mail"`, `AUTH_TYPE = "oauth2_code"`. Exposes 15 public async methods. Connects to Microsoft Outlook mail via the Microsoft Graph v1.0 API. Read, search, send, reply, forward, move, and organise mail in folders for the signed-in user.

sections.title: Quick Start
sections.content:
1. Provision credentials in the Microsoft Outlook (Mail) dashboard.
2. In ACP → Manage Connectors → Microsoft Outlook (Mail), fill in the install fields below.
3. Click Install — the connector validates required config keys via `install()`.
4. The gateway calls `health_check()` to confirm credentials are valid.

sections.title: Authentication
sections.content:
Auth type: **oauth2_code** (Microsoft Identity Authorization Code grant + refresh_token).

1. Caller redirects the end-user to `auth_url` with `client_id`, `redirect_uri`, requested `scope` (`Mail.Read Mail.Send Mail.ReadWrite offline_access`), and a `state` value.
2. Microsoft redirects back to `redirect_uri` with `?code=...&state=...`.
3. Connector calls `authorize(code)` which POSTs `grant_type=authorization_code` to `token_url` and stores the returned `TokenInfo` (access_token, refresh_token, expires_at).
4. On any 401 the HTTP client transparently calls the refresher (`grant_type=refresh_token`), persists the new token via `set_token()`, and replays the original request with the new bearer token.

sections.title: Configuration
sections.content:
Install fields (per-tenant):

| Key | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `client_id` | string | yes | Found in Azure Portal → App registrations → your app → Overview → Application (client) ID. |
| `client_secret` | secret | yes | Generate under Azure Portal → App registrations → your app → Certificates & secrets → New client secret. |
| `tenant_id` | string | no | Use 'common' for multi-tenant, your Azure tenant ID for single-tenant |
| `scopes` | string | no | Space-separated Graph delegated permissions. 'offline_access' is required to receive a refresh token. |
| `auth_url` | string | no | Microsoft Identity authorization endpoint. Swap 'common' with your tenant ID for single-tenant. |
| `token_url` | string | no | Microsoft Identity token endpoint. Swap 'common' with your tenant ID for single-tenant. |
| `base_url` | string | no | Base URL for the Microsoft Graph mail API. Leave blank to use v1.0. |
| `rate_limit_per_min` | number | no | Soft cap on outbound Graph requests per minute. Graph applies its own throttling; this guards against bursts. |

sections.title: API Methods
sections.content:
15 public async methods. See `metadata/connector.json::apis` for full param + return docs.

### `async install()`
Validates client_id/client_secret and persists install-time config. Call before OAuth authorization.
### `async authorize(auth_code, state)`
Exchanges an OAuth2 authorization code for access + refresh tokens via the Microsoft Identity token endpoint.
### `async health_check()`
Verifies Microsoft Graph reachability by fetching /me for the signed-in user.
### `async list_messages(folder, top, skip, filter, search)`
Lists messages in a mail folder via /me/mailFolders/{folder}/messages with optional $filter and $search.
### `async get_message(message_id)`
Fetches a single message via /me/messages/{id}.
### `async send_mail(to, subject, body, body_type, cc, bcc, attachments)`
Sends mail via /me/sendMail. Returns {} on 202 Accepted. Requires Mail.Send.
### `async create_draft(to, subject, body)`
Creates a draft message via POST /me/messages without sending.
### `async reply_message(message_id, comment)`
Replies to a message via POST /me/messages/{id}/reply with a comment block.
### `async forward_message(message_id, to, comment)`
Forwards a message via POST /me/messages/{id}/forward to a list of recipients.
### `async move_message(message_id, destination_folder_id)`
Moves a message to another folder via POST /me/messages/{id}/move.
### `async delete_message(message_id)`
Deletes a message via DELETE /me/messages/{id}.
### `async list_mail_folders()`
Lists the signed-in user's mail folders via /me/mailFolders.
### `async create_mail_folder(display_name)`
Creates a new mail folder via POST /me/mailFolders.
### `async mark_as_read(message_id, is_read)`
Patches a message's isRead flag via PATCH /me/messages/{id}.
### `async search_messages(query, top)`
Runs a $search query across the user's mail via /me/messages?$search="…".

sections.title: Error Handling
sections.content:
Common HTTP status mapping (via `_STATUS_MAP` or `_classify_failure`):

| Status | ConnectorHealth | AuthStatus |
|---|---|---|
| 401 | OFFLINE | TOKEN_EXPIRED |
| 403 | UNHEALTHY | INVALID_CREDENTIALS |
| 429 | DEGRADED | CONNECTED (logged) |
| 5xx | UNHEALTHY | FAILED (retried) |

sections.title: Troubleshooting
sections.content:
| Symptom | Fix |
|---|---|
| `MISSING_CREDENTIALS` at install | Verify all required install fields populated |
| `INVALID_CREDENTIALS` at health_check | Confirm API user has required permissions in the Microsoft Outlook (Mail) dashboard |
| 429 rate limit warnings | The client honours `Retry-After`; reduce request frequency |
